methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $63M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Bedrock Ocean is a Series A–stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Anthony DiMare and Charles Chiau. The company has raised $63M to date. The absence of viable public comparables and a limited set of private peers in the ocean tech sector constrains the depth of available benchmarking; consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology rather than multiples-based triangulation.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $63M total capital raised as a baseline proxy for current value, reflecting typical venture step-ups between Series A and the next institutional funding round. This approach yields a midpoint valuation of $126M. Adjusting for reasonable variance around this estimate—accounting for execution risk, market conditions, and the relative illiquidity of the frontier-tech ocean sector—produces a valuation range of $107M (low) to $145M (high).

The narrow methodological toolkit and absence of public or established private comps introduce material uncertainty into this range. Key variables affecting final valuation include deployment progress, customer acquisition trajectory, regulatory pathway clarity, and competitive positioning within the nascent ocean technology market. A more robust analysis would benefit from inclusion of revenue multiples or asset-based metrics once operational scale becomes measurable. Until then, the $107M–$145M range represents the defensible boundaries supported by stage-based analysis alone.
